title: Image-to-Image Diffusion
concept: Image-to-image diffusion starts the reverse process partway through (from a partially noised version of the real image) instead of from pure noise.

exercises.prompt: When you use a photo filter app to turn your selfie into a 'painting' version, what stays mostly the same?

options.text: The overall shapes and layout of your face and background
Absolutely nothing, it's a totally random new picture
Only the colors of the sky

exercises.explain: Filters keep the structure of your original photo but change its style — that's the same idea behind image-to-image diffusion!

cards.title: Starting From a Real Photo
cards.body: What if an AI didn't have to start from total static noise every time? What if it could start from YOUR photo, add a bit of noise, then 'un-noise' it into something new — while keeping your photo's shape and layout? That's exactly what image-to-image diffusion does!

cards.title: What Is Image-to-Image Diffusion?
cards.body: Image-to-image diffusion is a way of generating pictures where the AI doesn't start from pure random noise. Instead, it starts with a real image, adds some noise to it partway, and then runs the reverse (denoising) process from that halfway point — so the result keeps the original image's structure but changes its style or details.

cards.title: Renovating a House
cards.body: Regular diffusion is like building a house from an empty lot — starting with nothing but dirt. Image-to-image diffusion is like renovating an existing house: you keep the walls and layout (the structure) but change the paint, furniture, and decorations (the style). You don't tear it down and start over!

cards.title: Turning a Sketch Into a Painting
cards.body: Imagine you draw a rough sketch of a mountain landscape. You feed it into an image-to-image diffusion model, add some noise, and ask for 'oil painting style.' The AI denoises it, keeping the mountains and sky in the same places, but now it looks like a rich, textured oil painting instead of a pencil sketch.

cards.title: Myth: It Always Starts From Nothing
cards.body: Myth: Every diffusion model must start from pure random noise. Truth: Image-to-image diffusion starts from a REAL image with only partial noise added, so the final result stays connected to the original picture's structure instead of being a completely new random creation.

cards.title: Text-to-Image vs Image-to-Image
cards.body: Both use the same denoising idea, but they start from very different places!

left.text: Starts from 100% pure random noise, guided only by a text prompt to build an image from scratch

right.text: Starts from a real image with partial noise added, keeping its structure while changing style or details

exercises.prompt: What does image-to-image diffusion start with, instead of pure noise?

options.text: A partially noised version of a real image
A video instead of an image

exercises.explain: Image-to-image diffusion begins with a real image that has had some (not all) noise added, so the reverse process starts partway through.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Image-to-image diffusion keeps the general structure of the original image while changing its style.
exercises.explain: True! Because it starts from a partially noised real image rather than pure noise, the underlying layout and shapes tend to stay recognizable.

exercises.sentence: Image-to-image diffusion starts the reverse process from a ___ noised real image instead of pure noise.
exercises.explain: The key idea is 'partially' — only some noise is added, so the process starts partway through instead of from scratch.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario best describes image-to-image diffusion in action?

options.text: An artist uploads their own drawing, adds some noise, and asks the AI to restyle it into a watercolor painting while keeping the same layout
An AI generates a picture of a dragon purely from the text prompt 'a red dragon flying,' with no starting image at all
An AI randomly picks colors and shapes with no starting image or noise process involved

exercises.explain: Only the first option starts from a real existing image with added noise — that's the defining feature of image-to-image diffusion.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: Why does image-to-image diffusion add only PARTIAL noise to the original image, instead of turning it into complete random noise like text-to-image models do?

options.text: Because starting from partial noise lets the reverse process preserve the original image's structure while still allowing style changes
Because full noise would make the AI run out of computer memory
Because partial noise makes the image file smaller
Because AI models cannot handle full noise at all

exercises.explain: The whole point of image-to-image diffusion is to keep some 'memory' of the original picture's layout. Full noise would erase all that structure, turning it into a totally new image — which is what text-to-image does instead.
